config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu ditempatkan di atas segalanya, itu mudah disalahgunakan untuk menekan kelompok kecil atau orang yang tidak berdaya. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai tanpa mengorbankan hak dasar seperti kebebasan, martabat, dan perlakuan adil. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dilindungi dengan kuat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to justify trampling minority rights and personal freedom. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are absolute in every case, since laws against violence, fraud, and public harm exist for a reason. The real standard should be that society can limit individual action only when there is a clear, narrow, and justified public need, not just because the majority wants it. When people say "the common good" without strong limits, I get worried that the rights of unpopular people will be the first thing sacrificed.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman, tekanan sosial, atau pengabaian terhadap kelompok kecil yang tidak punya kuasa. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi pembatasannya harus benar-benar sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa itu nyaman. Kepentingan sosial yang sehat justru lahir kalau hak dasar orang tetap dilindungi, bukan dikorbankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically outrank the rights of the individual, because in the United States that logic has too often been used to excuse abuse by majorities and governments. I do believe society can override some individual choices in limited cases, like preventing violence, fraud, or direct harm to others, but that has to be tightly justified and narrowly applied. What I reject is the idea that a vague "common good" can be used to erase unpopular people’s rights whenever it is convenient. In my view, a healthy society is one that protects individual rights first and only limits them when there is a clear, specific, and serious public necessity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh diprioritaskan secara umum di atas hak individu, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi ketertiban” atau “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara minoritas dan orang yang lemah. Saya setuju bahwa ada keadaan tertentu ketika hak individu bisa dibatasi, misalnya untuk mencegah kekerasan atau bahaya nyata bagi orang lain, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sangat jelas, sempit, dan tidak boleh jadi alat mayoritas untuk memaksakan kehendak. Kalau hak dasar mudah dikorbankan atas nama masyarakat, yang terjadi bukan kebaikan bersama, melainkan penyalahgunaan kuasa yang dibungkus bahasa moral. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang benar-benar sehat justru dibangun dari perlindungan kuat terhadap hak individu, lalu kepentingan publik disusun tanpa menginjak martabat orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, that idea has too often been the excuse for majorities, politicians, or institutions to silence unpopular people and weaken civil liberties in the name of order or the common good. I do think society can limit individual rights in narrow cases, like stopping violence or fraud, but those limits need to be specific, necessary, and tightly controlled. If “society first” becomes a blank check, then the people most likely to lose are usually the ones with the least power.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
